items.question: What is VarLens designed for?
items.answer: VarLens is an offline desktop application designed for research-oriented exploration of genomic variant data. It is not intended for clinical diagnostic use.

items.question: Who should use this tool?
items.answer: VarLens is designed for researchers, geneticists, and bioinformaticians with training in genomics and variant interpretation.

items.question: What are the system requirements?
items.answer: VarLens runs as a desktop application on Windows, macOS, and Linux. No internet connection is required for analysis — all processing occurs locally on your machine.

items.question: What file formats are supported?
items.answer: VarLens imports gzipped JSON files generated by the Ensembl Variant Effect Predictor (VEP) annotation pipeline.

items.question: How is my data stored?
items.answer: All data is stored in a local SQLite database on your machine. No data is transmitted externally or shared with third parties.

items.question: Can I export filtered results?
items.answer: Yes, you can export your filtered variant data to Excel or CSV format directly from the variant table.

items.question: How are pathogenicity classifications determined?
items.answer: Classifications are based on imported annotation data from sources such as CADD scores, gnomAD frequencies, and predicted variant consequences. These classifications are informational only and must not be used for clinical decision-making.

items.question: Why might results differ from other tools?
items.answer: Differences can arise from varying annotation sources, database versions, and classification algorithms. Always verify findings with independent methods.

items.question: What are the known limitations?
items.answer: Variant annotations reflect the quality and completeness of their source databases. VarLens has not been validated for clinical use and currently supports single-variant analysis only.

items.question: What should I do if I find an error?
items.answer: Please report any issues through the VarLens GitHub repository. We recommend verifying unexpected results with independent tools and databases.

items.question: Is my data shared with anyone?
items.answer: No. All data remains on your local machine. VarLens does not collect, transmit, or share any user data or genomic information.

items.question: What happens to data when I close the app?
items.answer: Your data persists in the local SQLite database between sessions. Data is only removed when you explicitly delete a case.
